summary: Linked the Koopa flame item translation by switching `melee/it/items/itkoopaflame.c` from NonMatching to Matching in `configure.py`. The source changes were small but targeted: use `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj` instead of `GET_JOBJ` in the animation routine, adjust collision code to preserve/use a copied flags value, and move `it_2725_Logic111_Absorbed` earlier in the file. PR body credits Chippy for figuring out the `_Coll` match.
what_changed: Changed `Object(NonMatching, "melee/it/items/itkoopaflame.c")` to `Object(Matching, "melee/it/items/itkoopaflame.c")` in `configure.py`, making the file part of the matching build set.
In `itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Anim`, replaced `GET_JOBJ(gobj)` with `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj(gobj)` and left an inline note that `GET_JOBJ` does not work there.

In `itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Coll`, introduced a second integer variable `flags2` and used assignment in the condition `if ((flags2 = flags) != 0)` before calling `itKoopaFlame_Update_Direction` and `itKoopaFlame_Update_Angle`.

Moved the definition of `it_2725_Logic111_Absorbed` from after `it_2725_Logic111_EvtUnk` to before `it_2725_Logic111_ShieldBounced` without changing its body: it calls `efLib_DestroyAll(gobj)` and returns true.
smart_moves: The `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj` substitution is a precise API choice for matching: even if `GET_JOBJ` appears semantically equivalent in many contexts, this call site needed the direct getter to match or behave correctly.

The collision fix keeps a copied flag value (`flags2`) through the nonzero branch, suggesting the original compiler/register allocation or generated code required an explicit temporary rather than reusing `flags` directly.
Function reordering was used as a low-risk matching/linking tactic: the absorbed handler logic stayed unchanged while its source position changed to satisfy object layout/order constraints.
The PR kept the matching delta minimal, touching only `configure.py` and the item source necessary to link `itkoopaflame.c`.
decomp_lessons: Do not assume helper macros like `GET_JOBJ` are always interchangeable with the underlying HSD object getter; inspect the expected codegen or call behavior for each item routine.

For item collision routines, small-looking temporaries can matter for matching. Introducing an explicit copied flags variable may be necessary to reproduce compiler output around conditionals and repeated calls.
When a file is nearly matched, declaration/definition order can be part of the matching problem even if function bodies are already correct.
After resolving the last nonmatching routines, remember to update `configure.py` from `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Matching, ...)` so the project actually links the matched translation unit.
naming_conventions: Item source path uses lowercase item name: `src/melee/it/items/itkoopaflame.c`.

Koopa flame routines use the `itKoopaFlame_` prefix for named local behavior functions such as `itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Anim`, `itKoopaFlame_UnkMotion0_Coll`, `itKoopaFlame_Update_Direction`, and `itKoopaFlame_Update_Angle`.
Logic callback functions retain address/logic-number style names such as `it_2725_Logic111_Clanked`, `it_2725_Logic111_Absorbed`, `it_2725_Logic111_ShieldBounced`, and `it_2725_Logic111_EvtUnk`.
Collision flag enum/constant naming in the excerpt includes `Collide_RightWallMask` and item-specific `itkpf_RightWall`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj(gobj)` rather than `GET_JOBJ(gobj)` when the macro expansion or type-specific helper does not match the target for a given item object.
Preserve an intermediate collision flags value with an explicit assignment expression in the branch condition: `if ((flags2 = flags) != 0)`.
Reorder function definitions to match expected object layout without changing logic when body-level matching is already correct.
Convert the object to `Matching` in `configure.py` only after the source compiles/links as matched.
review_feedback: No formal review comments were present in the provided slice.
PR body says: "thanks Chippy for figuring out _Coll!", indicating the collision routine was the notable or difficult matching piece and crediting Chippy for that work.
follow_up_queries: Find other item PRs where `GET_JOBJ` was replaced with `HSD_GObjGetHSDObj` for matching or correctness.
Search for collision routines using an explicit copied flags variable or assignment-in-condition pattern like `if ((flags2 = flags) != 0)`.
Compare other `Logic111` item callback ordering patterns to see whether absorbed/shield-bounced/event callback order is recurring matching-sensitive layout.
Look for additional PRs crediting Chippy on `_Coll` routines to reuse collision matching tactics.
